relationship_corrections.reason: A BloodUnit may optionally be associated with a single Actor (e.g., donor), but not necessarily many. One-to-many is too restrictive.
A BloodUnit should have an optional association with one Actor, not mandatory one-to-one. Current cardinality is too strict.
Cardinality '2' is non-standard; an Operation likely relates to a single State (e.g., current state). Changed to one.
REQ_BBT_01 is a requirement identifier, not a domain class. This relationship should not exist in a domain model.

architecture_corrections.description: Clinical_Team, Inventory_Manager, and Quality_Assurance use association (--|>) instead of inheritance (--|>) to Actor.
TransfusionRequestData is not associated with Reservation; a reservation links a transfusion request to blood units.
REQ_BBT_01 appears to be a placeholder or requirement artifact, not a domain class; it has no defined role or attributes.

architecture_suggestions.suggestion: Rename to use consistent naming convention: e.g., BloodUnitRepository, TransfusionRequestRepository to follow ubiquitous language of repositories.
Consider introducing a value object for ABO/Rh blood type (e.g., BloodTypeVO) to encapsulate compatibility logic.
Consider making BloodUnit an aggregate root to manage its state (available, reserved, expired) and lifecycle, and have Reservation reference it via ID.
